title: english annual update 2018-19
text:
English Annual Update 2017-2018
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
The English Department has had a busy year. We served 1057 students in our English
courses during the 2017-18 academic year. Incredibly, this is 78.3% of our locally
produced FTES.
We have also been busy planning for the instructional changes required by
AB705.These changes include:
• Fall 2017: Sponsored an English Department Curriculum Summit for full and PT
instructors, which included grade normalization, and information regarding
acceleration principles and curriculum expectations.
• End of Fall 2017: Hired new FT English Instructor to replace Steve Reynolds.
• Spring 2018: implemented a lab hour to English 1001 to improve contact hours
and promote student success. Preliminary data shows a slight increase in
success rates from Spring 2017 to Spring 2018.
• Spring/Summer 2018: FT and PT faculty attended professional development
activities including CAP and Reading Apprenticeship institutes.
• Spring 2018: Initiated discussions for future revisions for ENGL 1001 to meet
AB705 requirements and timelines.
• Spring 2018: submitted COR revisions to move non-credit ESL courses from
ENGL to ESL categories to align with minimum qualifications for the ESL
discipline.
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
English department faculty co-chaired the committee that was responsible for creating
the new SLO assessment process and manual which will be implemented in the 18-19
academic year. Faculty continued to assess course level SLOs through formal course
level assessment activities (essays, exams, quizzes, etc) during the 17-18 academic
year, and documentation of course level SLO achievement is reflected in the course
grades submitted by English faculty.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
The English department was well supported in their professional development activities
through BSI/BSSOT allocations, and feels this level of support greatly contributed to our
ability to quickly and effectively respond to the changing curriculum and legislative
landscape.
The lack of communication and connection to the ASC proved problematic in our initial
curriculum and support discussions. The ASC lacked sufficient services and operational
hours necessary to create a thriving learning environment for our students, and even
directly impacted course lab time.
In the Fall 2017, we were negatively impacted by the departure of Steve Reynolds until
the mid-year hire of Elizabeth Carlyle.

-- 1 of 2 --

4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
• ASC Coordinator/English Instructor, FT
• Professional Development funding including travel funds
• Memberships to NADE, NCTE, CCC
(Submit a CQIP if you are requesting a budget allocation that falls outside of regular
yearly budget allocations).

-- 2 of 2 --
